exemplar.title: What challenges are raised by the dissemination and/or communication of knowledge?

detailedExemplarData.title: What challenges are raised by the dissemination and/or communication of knowledge?

strengths: Three specific real-world objects are clearly identified with precise contexts (Hindu swastika, Gerber logo, Taylor Swift deepfake article).
Explicit links are made between each object and the chosen prompt about challenges in knowledge dissemination.
Many claims are supported by concrete examples and citations, giving adequate justification for each objectâs inclusion.
weaknesses: Commentary is largely descriptive and lacks deeper TOK analysis using key concepts (e.g., interpretation, authority).
Some evidence relies on weak or anecdotal sources and statistics without critical evaluation of credibility.
Citation style is inconsistent and the work would benefit from an explicit knowledge question and more precise TOK terminology.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition meets the IB descriptor for the 5â6 band: three objects and contexts are identified, links to the prompt are explained, and justifications are supported by evidence. However, the analysis remains more explanatory than analytical, TOK vocabulary is underused, and some sources lack rigor. These factors prevent it from reaching the 7â8 band, but the clear structure, relevant examples and overall coherence justify awarding the top of the 5â6 band (6/10).

annotations.comment: The prompt is clearly stated, but the student could formulate an explicit knowledge question to focus the exhibitionâs analytical direction.
The object title is clear, but the student could specify the exact location and date of the symbolâs installation for more precise context.
The narrative description effectively situates the object, but it remains largely descriptive. The student should draw on TOK concepts (e.g. interpretation, perspective) to deepen analysis.
Good etymological context is provided for the swastika, illustrating its original meaning. The student could link this to how language shapes understanding.
The historical overview and citation from the Holocaust Memorial Museum and Britannica strengthen the real-world context of the swastikaâs evolving meaning.
The justification for inclusion starts but is cut off mid-sentence. The student should complete this thought and tie it back explicitly to the promptâs challenges.
The distinction between the Nazi Hakenkreuz and the Hindu swastika is well described. To enhance the TOK angle, the student could analyze how public perception overrides factual distinctions.
The Gerber section header is clear, but the student should include a brief rationale here to signal the objectâs relevance before diving into history.
Including the product image is effective, but the student should caption it with date and source to anchor the real-world context.
The raw URL interrupting the narrative is distracting. The student should integrate the reference more fluidly into the commentary.
The historical background of Gerber is informative but remains descriptive. The student should connect these facts to the challenges of conveying brand knowledge across cultures.
The link to the prompt is stated, but the commentary would benefit from explicit TOK vocabulary (e.g. "translation as a way of knowing").
The French and Quebec mistranslation example is interesting but relies on a single source. The student should corroborate the anecdote with additional evidence.
The justification restates the objectâs challenge but lacks a precise link back to the IA prompt. The student should explicitly reference the dissemination aspect.
The header for Object 3 is generic. The student should label it with the specific article title to clarify the real-world source.
Including the AP News article image is effective. To strengthen context, add a caption with publication date and author.
The raw URL disrupts the flow. The student should paraphrase the link and integrate it as a proper citation.
The opening description locates the deepfake article, but could more strongly frame how AI challenges the reliability of evidence.
The explanation of deepfakes is accurate but descriptive. The student should analyze how this technology undermines shared knowledge frameworks.
Reframing the description of âunrealâ as ârealâ into a knowledge question would sharpen the TOK focus on appearance versus reality.
The impact narrative is compelling but drifts into moral commentary. The student should tie each effect back to the promptâs theme of knowledge dissemination.
The inclusion rationale articulates relevance to teenagers, but should explicitly reference the promptâs wording for cohesion.
The statistic that 95% of deepfakes are non-consensual is dramatic but sourced to a blog platform. The student should evaluate source credibility.
The final paragraph summarizes societal impact well but lacks a unifying TOK perspective comparing all three objects.
Footnotes use varied formats; the student should adopt a consistent citation style (e.g., MLA or APA) to strengthen scholarly rigor.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?

three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images

highly generic images of

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
